summary: Systems architecture review accepts the M6 MVP Spec generation slice. The implementation keeps the MVP Spec agent as a pure generation boundary over the persisted Opportunity detail view model, keeps DB loading/upsert/serialization ownership in lib/opportunities.ts, exposes only the nested POST /api/opportunities/{id}/mvp-spec route, upserts the existing MvpSpec record by opportunityId, returns JSON-safe markdown/model/timestamps, and integrates the detail-page Generate/Regenerate/Copy panel without schema, package, provider, env, auth, scan, scoring, status, list, or dashboard expansion.

notes: Status wrapper showed product changes limited to README.md, agents/index.ts, agents/mvp-spec-agent.ts, app/api/opportunities/[id]/mvp-spec/**, components/MvpSpecPanel.tsx, components/OpportunityDetail.tsx, lib/opportunities.ts, tests/mvp-spec-agent.test.ts, tests/mvp-spec-api.test.ts, and tests/opportunities.test.ts.
The implementation handoff under project/messages/outbox is JustGoal coordination state requested by this workflow, not product implementation creep.

A protected-path diff over package.json, package-lock.json, prisma/**, lib/db.ts, lib/env.ts, lib/radar-tasks.ts, lib/scoring.ts, lib/schemas.ts, services/**, scan APIs, radar-task APIs, the existing opportunity status API files, list/dashboard/root pages, app shell/global CSS, docs/**, mock-data/**, skills/**, .codex/**, and .agents/** produced no product diff.

evidence: agents/mvp-spec-agent.ts:43 defines MvpSpecOpportunityInputSchema around detail-view fields such as keyword, title, targetUser, scoreBreakdownItems, toolConcept, and killCriteria rather than raw Prisma rows.
lib/opportunities.ts:415 serializes the persisted opportunity through serializeOpportunityDetail() before calling the generator, so the agent receives the persisted Opportunity detail view model.

agents/mvp-spec-agent.ts:111 defaults generateMvpSpec() to deterministic Markdown when no llmClient is injected; agents/mvp-spec-agent.ts:125 uses safeJsonCompletion only through explicit injection/default utility and does not construct providers or read env.
lib/opportunities.ts:400 owns generateAndPersistMvpSpec(), including id parsing, persisted opportunity loading, generation, upsert, and serialization.
app/api/opportunities/[id]/mvp-spec/_api.ts:85 keeps the route handler injectable and thin; app/api/opportunities/[id]/mvp-spec/route.ts:3 exports only POST.
components/MvpSpecPanel.tsx:30 calls the route with fetch but imports no Prisma/db helper and performs no persistence directly.
lib/opportunities.ts:420 calls dbClient.mvpSpec.upsert with where: { opportunityId: id } against the existing unique field.
lib/opportunities.ts:423 and lib/opportunities.ts:428 persist only opportunityId, markdown, and generatedByModel on create/update.
lib/opportunities.ts:24 defines a narrow MvpSpec select for id, opportunityId, markdown, generatedByModel, createdAt, and updatedAt.
lib/opportunities.ts:33 extends the detail include with mvpSpec select without changing list/dashboard includes.
lib/opportunities.ts:390 loads detail pages with opportunityDetailInclude, so reopening /opportunities/{id} can display an existing persisted spec.
lib/opportunities.ts:922 serializes MvpSpec rows into plain JSON-safe strings with ISO createdAt and updatedAt values.
lib/opportunities.ts:637 adds mvpSpec to OpportunityDetailViewModel through serializeMvpSpec(getMvpSpecFromOpportunity(opportunity)).
app/api/opportunities/[id]/mvp-spec/_api.ts:49 maps missing/malformed ids and missing opportunities to sanitized NOT_FOUND.
app/api/opportunities/[id]/mvp-spec/_api.ts:53 maps MvpSpecGenerationValidationError to sanitized AGENT_VALIDATION_ERROR.

app/api/opportunities/[id]/mvp-spec/_api.ts:61 maps Zod errors to issues without raw input payload text, and app/api/opportunities/[id]/mvp-spec/_api.ts:75 logs unexpected errors server-side while returning a generic INTERNAL_ERROR.
components/OpportunityDetail.tsx:17 builds only the nested mvp-spec URL alongside the existing status URL.
components/OpportunityDetail.tsx:140 inserts MvpSpecPanel with opportunity.mvpSpec, leaving status controls, score sections, list pages, and dashboard pages untouched.

components/MvpSpecPanel.tsx:18 keeps generated spec state local to the panel and components/MvpSpecPanel.tsx:98 exposes Generate/Regenerate while components/MvpSpecPanel.tsx:107 exposes Copy Markdown.

Searches over the changed M6 surface found no process.env reads, provider construction, auth/session wiring, scan orchestration call, scoring formula call, status mutation change, list/dashboard route change, package change, or Prisma schema change.

README.md:356 documents the default MVP Spec path as deterministic/local without provider/env/network/package requirements, and README.md:364 lists schema/provider/env/auth/scan/scoring/package/deployment non-goals.

decisions: ACCEPT: The agent input boundary is the persisted Opportunity detail view model and not Prisma rows or raw scan objects.
ACCEPT: The route remains under app/api/opportunities/[id]/mvp-spec and the existing app/api/opportunities/[id]/ status files are not changed.
ACCEPT: Persistence and serialization ownership remain in lib/opportunities.ts, with no DB writes in the agent or client UI.
ACCEPT: MvpSpec persistence uses upsert by opportunityId and returns a JSON-safe view model with markdown, generatedByModel, createdAt, and updatedAt.
ACCEPT: Detail serialization loads and displays an already-generated persisted spec.
ACCEPT: The diff stays within the M6 scope and avoids forbidden schema/package/provider/env/auth/scan/scoring/status/list/dashboard expansion.
risks: The endpoint remains unauthenticated under the documented local single-admin MVP assumption; public deployment still needs a later auth/session/ownership slice.
The deterministic Markdown is intentionally template-based. It satisfies the systems boundary, but domain usefulness should still be reviewed by the domain_designer gate.

generateAndPersistMvpSpec() validates injected generated output for non-empty markdown/model before persistence; the production default generator enforces the required section schema, but a future custom injected generator should preserve that stronger validation contract deliberately.
The UI renders full Markdown in-page for copyability. Very large future specs may need a more compact preview, but the current panel is bounded by max height and remains acceptable for this MVP.
